span.p: Does the metric reflect value the customer actually gets - not just activity

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Decline - it measures output not outcome - explain why to the proposer

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Can a team directly move this metric with their work

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: North Star candidate - pair with input metrics that a team can move

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is this metric already captured by an existing dashboard metric

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Decline - redundant - point to the existing metric; consolidate if definitions differ

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Does adding it risk creating a metric proliferation problem - more than 5 to 7 key metrics

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Replace one existing metric with the new one - or decline until a slot opens

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Add with a guardrail pairing - name the metric it must not harm
